title: Maximum Subarray Sum
problem: Given an array of n integers, find the contiguous subarray with the largest sum and return that sum.

title: Median of Two Sorted Arrays
problem: Given two sorted arrays nums1 and nums2 of size m and n, return the median of the two sorted arrays in O(log(m+n)) time.

title: Division using Binary Search
problem: Implement integer division of two numbers without using the division, multiplication, or modulo operators, using binary search on the answer.

title: Maximum Product Triplet
problem: Find three integers in an array whose product is maximum, considering both large positives and pairs of large negatives.

title: 3n + 1 Problem (Collatz Sequence)
problem: For a given range [i, j], find the maximum cycle length of the Collatz sequence for any starting number in that range.

title: Euclid Problem
problem: Find the GCD and LCM of two numbers using the Euclidean algorithm and extend it to find Bezout coefficients.

title: Tug of War
problem: Split a set of n numbers into two subsets of size n/2 each such that the difference of subset sums is minimized, using backtracking.

title: Wildcard String Matching
problem: Implement pattern matching that supports '?' (matches any single character) and '*' (matches any sequence of characters, including empty).

title: Rotten Oranges
problem: Given a grid of fresh and rotten oranges, find the minimum minutes until no fresh orange remains, using multi-source BFS.

title: Minimum Cost Path
problem: Find the minimum cost path from the top-left to the bottom-right of a cost matrix, moving only right, down, or diagonally.

title: 15 Puzzle
problem: Given a 4x4 sliding puzzle board, determine the minimum number of moves to reach the goal state using IDA* or BFS with heuristics.
constraints: Board is always a valid permutation of 0-15

title: First Set Bit
problem: Given an integer, find the position of the first (lowest) set bit using bit manipulation.

title: Coin Change
problem: Given coin denominations and a target amount, find the minimum number of coins needed to make that amount, or -1 if impossible.

title: Spiral Matrix
problem: Given an m x n matrix, return all elements of the matrix in spiral order.
